enableAgricultureHelp: Solar radiation, CAPE, soil temperature, irradiance under *.agriculture
enableComfortHelp: Heat index, wind chill, humidex, UV index under *.comfort
hourlyDaysExceedsDaysCount: Must not exceed number of forecast days
hourlyDaysHighWarning: Each hourly day generates many datapoints depending on enabled options
iconSetCustomHint:
Upload to: Admin → Files → openmeteo-notify.0/icons/custom/
Required day icons (24):
wmo_00  wmo_01  wmo_02  wmo_03  wmo_45  wmo_48
wmo_51  wmo_53  wmo_55  wmo_61  wmo_63  wmo_65
wmo_71  wmo_73  wmo_75  wmo_77  wmo_80  wmo_81
wmo_82  wmo_85  wmo_86  wmo_95  wmo_96  wmo_99
Night icons optional (suffix n): wmo_00n.svg, wmo_01n.svg …

Note: Icons are stored as Base64 strings inside the widget datapoint.
Please use pure vector SVGs where possible. If your SVGs contain embedded
pixel images, choose the lowest resolution possible.
warnOfficialMinLevelHelp: Only send notification from this warning level onwards
warnLevelDesc1: All warnings – including preliminary information and notices
warnLevelDesc2: Standard warnings and above – e.g. frost, wind, slippery roads
warnLevelDesc3: Severe warnings and above – e.g. storm, heavy rain, severe weather
warnLevelDesc4: Extreme warnings only – highest danger level
warnExcludeKeywordsHelp: Comma-separated keywords – warnings containing any of these will not trigger a notification
warnIntervalMinutesHelp: How often DWD / MeteoAlarm is queried – independent of the weather update interval
widgetDetailedNoAstronomy: Astronomy data is disabled – moon phases will not be shown in the detailed widget. Enable Astronomy in General settings.
widgetDetailedNoHourly: Hourly data is disabled – the detailed widget will show no hourly section. Enable hourly days in General settings.
widgetDetailedPartialHourly: Hourly data is only available for %s day(s) – remaining days show daily data only. Increase hourly days in General settings to cover more days.
warnNotifyLiftHelp: Sends a notification when a previously reported warning is cancelled or expires
widgetHint: Each widget creates an HTML datapoint that can be embedded directly in VIS or Jarvis.
